payload.answer: Treat this as a temporal status transition for the same project. Preserve the new fact as current and do not present the older planned/open status as current.
citations: Existing: "The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open"
New: "The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented"

entity_resolution.reason: The new statement refers to the same project as the existing state, with added scope detail (MVP) and a newer status.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented.

details: Detected same underlying entity despite punctuation/wording variation.
Classified as temporal progression rather than contradiction.
Recommended updating durable memory so only the newer status is current.

repair_options: Update the existing project record to mark the prior planned/open status as superseded by the newer implemented MVP status.
Store the implementation as a dated status transition or milestone rather than as a parallel current status.
If schema allows, keep both facts with temporal ordering: earlier planned/open -> later MVP implemented.
